title: Omics-availability map per model (expression/CN/mutations-open/fusions/drug)
context: Researchers need to know which open omics data types exist for each model (expression, copy-number, open mutations, fusions, drug) without the catalog recomputing or re-hosting raw data. This is an availability map of what authoritative resources already publish. Standing guardrails bind this task: open-access sources only (no dbGaP/EGA/biobank/controlled-access); COSMIC/OncoKB are link-only; donor fields are published-only with no re-identification or external linkage; every assertion carries field-level provenance; the deliverable is de-identified research metadata, never patient data and never a clinical/therapeutic recommendation.
objective: Build the per-model omics-availability map recording which open data types each model has, with provenance.
acceptanceCriteria: For each catalogued model, records availability (present/absent + link) for expression, copy-number, open mutations, fusions, and drug-response data types.
Availability reflects only what authoritative open resources already publish; no raw data is recomputed or re-hosted; controlled-access types marked unavailable/EXCLUDE.
Each availability assertion is field-level provenanced (source + version + link); COSMIC/OncoKB entries are link-only.
Deliverable dataset licensed CC-BY-4.0; validates in CI.
resources: ICLAC Register of Misidentified Cell Lines
